review_text: My family have stayed here several times. Most recently we were welcomed by Hayley. She is an excellent addition and always goes above and beyond, with a big smile on her dial! Thank you and see you again soon!
Good location, price and amenities

We are currently travelling around Australia and on several occasions have needed to come home for family, friends and health issues. We have stayed many times at Bells Caravan Park. I just want to do a big shout out to Crystal. She is always happy  to help us nothing is too much trouble.
Absolutely beautiful place to stay

Stayed overnight. Asked for site away from road but none available.  So parked against fence line . Had someone checking us out during the day. At 2.30 am someone came back with bolt cutters cut the chains and took one of our E-bikes. Heaps of homeless in town. Won’t be returning. Staff were pleasant but wish they had of put on site in middle of park as there was a free spot.  As for road noise it was constant all night
Powered sites great weekly rates, great location, clean amenities and great camp kitchen

Brilliant caravan park. Manager is just awesome, really accomodating and so organised. Stayed twice now really great location opposite water. Clean facilities, nice size sites, great spot not far from entertainment centre, thanks again we will be back :)
Nice park except for the smell of sewerage. Would rate it more except thats not good. Plenty of green ants also.
Nice little caravan park really well located to all amenities
It's cheap during Xmas but there's nothing to beach across the road, rocky and walking paths. Secure your stuff  bikes and scooters, thiiehs about.ammities are clean
Quite convenient location for an overnighter when passing through Brisbane
Great little park, well priced and I highly recommend it.

Lovely quiet park, grounds well kept as are amenities. Only drawback, no pool 😁.

07/2025  -  after staying quite a few times we requested a refund of our deposit for this year as my husband has cancer & we are unable to travel. Offered a dr's cert. Told, no refund, that's council policy. This we knew but thought under the circumstances?? I doubt very much that the circumstances were even told to council as we've had no follow up. Last park I contacted was very sensitive to the situation & I received a refund. This park was in Warrnambool Vic. We've also managed to change our booking in the hope our situation changes, on the Gold Coast. Now that is good PR & service. Be careful everyone of the council parks.
Nice caravan park but a lot of  road noise
The road noise is annoying which we expected from the reviews. The amenities were not cleaned during our stay and smelled bad after two days. The cooking facilities were left dirty and unusable.
No help from some reception staff trying to accommodate you but Crystal came to rescue and got me in . Council trained staff don't do real hospitality
Great location
Staff are very friendly and helpful with most inquiries to help you on your way
Good location, close to bus stop and small shops

After being told from a friend about the location and quietness of the park itself, I booked in for our winter getaway.
After checking the park location and site map online I asked for a site away from the main road. The staff granted my request and allocated a site at the quieter end of the park which backs on to a park/ sporting fields.
My husband and I did a pre-arrival check and I was pleased with the site allocated.
Our stay was enjoyable in the peaceful surrounds, close to the beautiful bay and conveniences.
The amenities are cleaned daily, the staff very friendly and obliging.
Great location with bus and bike/walking tracks right at the door. Supermarket in walking distance

I thoroughly enjoyed my stay at Bells. The staff could not have been friendlier, the ablutions cleaner and the campervan parking better. I had delicious fish and chips from a place close by, and was able to walk on the promenade in the evening, at peace. I would advise anyo e to give them a try.
Great smaller caravan park, very clean, quiet and well looked after park. Fantastic location and enjoyed our stay. Would definitely recommend.
It stinks all the time from the sea drain & septic, it’s council operated & managment is hit & miss, mostly a big miss
Great location for a holiday.

We stay at Bells when we are in Brisbane  it's tidy and clean and the staff have been great to us and nothing is a problem. Thanks for your help this stay you were all very helpful thank you Geoff and Debby.

We enjoyed our stay there. Admittedly the one and only 'camping' spot for a tent is a wee spot next to the flashing light of the boom gate operating for vehicles directly next to the office (office night light is really bright too). Clearly, anybody with half a brain would realise campers would prefer somewhere a little more discreet, and from reading other negative reviews on this issue, obviously, this camp management couldn't care less.
The facilities were clean and well presented. There were two toilet blocks, particularly convenient when the morning cleaning times clashed when they were really needed.. and creating a line up at the only open toliet where men were wanting to take their morning dump... a segue to mention about the aroma from the dump point does seem to travel through the camp.
The Horibrooke Esplanade is a busy road if you're a light sleeper. To be fair, staff were helpful (reluctantly.. after they refused us a refund) and endeavoured to accommodate us.
Sadly, my stay was affected by the damage Albert had done to the beaches and water quality  - plus it rained often and hard@

Disappointing and Frustrating Experience

We rang two weeks ago to request a specific site that doesn’t back onto other caravans, as we have a rear-door van. We were told that they don’t book specific sites, and that it was first in, first served — and that if we arrived early enough, we could choose a site.

That turned out to be false.

We arrived early, but there was no one in the office and no answer on the phone. After waiting over an hour, we drove in, found an empty site that suited us, set up and finally relaxed.  They finally returned our phone call, 3 hours after ringing initially ringing them to check on our booking, to be told the site we were on was actually booked, and that we’d have to move to a site that backed onto other caravans — exactly what we were trying to avoid.

We were then refused a refund despite clearly explaining the situation.

Getting in contact with staff is extremely difficult. Phones aren’t answered, the site office doesn't seem be hardly manned, and it can take hours for someone to get back to you. The overall experience was stressful, and could have been easily avoided if the staff were upfront about site bookings and responsive and actually on site.

I’ll be emailing Moreton Bay Council and Escape Parks Management to report this experience.

Be honest with your customers, clearly state what is and isn’t available, and let us decide if it works for us. Don’t mislead, and don’t make it harder than it needs to be.
I enjoyed staying at park for 2 nights my RV had mechanical problems and they found a nice space for me and when tow truck didn’t come until after 10am check out time I was not charged extra

Staff with bright pink hair is rude and obnoxious, will power trip for simple things and then talk down to you like a child.
Will not be back and would not recommend.

Dirty and broken amenities, dump site stinking out entire camp ground and rats the size of small dogs running around the bins.
All along the shoreline is tents and beat up old cars with the homeless living in them, not a nice area to be taking families.
Clean camp site facilities in a fairly good location

The management is rude and arrogant and there are a few areas that could really use improvement. The condition of the bathrooms and toilets was disappointing — they were in need of a thorough cleaning, especially with bleach, to bring them up to a more acceptable standard. The dump point could also do with a clean with bleach & disinfectant.

Additionally, I was concerned about the way the grounds are maintained around the vehicles. It’s important to note that our tow vehicles are quite valuable, and I would strongly suggest your staff avoid using whipper snippers near them, as it can cause stone chips. It would also make a big difference if your team used a catcher on the mower to prevent grass clippings from making the area look untidy.

I hope this feedback helps, as these changes could really improve the overall experience.

We picked ip our campervan and Bells was the first site we stayed in Australia. The site was in a good situation and very clean and tidy. The receptionist was friendly and very helpful to us. Would recommend this site.

This is my favourite place to stay in Brisbane.
The staff are lovely and the location is excellent. It's a really great waterfront to have a relaxing walk along.
Everything was ok except the noise from the street - we stayed 1 night and could not sleep.

A great traditional caravan park right across from Moreton Bay with nice sea breezes. Staff and management keep the grounds and amenities amazingly clean and tidy. Very friendly caravan park staff as well. Thanks team Bells CP.

I have stayed at this park several times and have enjoyed my stays. Staff are very friendly and helpful with all booking enquiries etc.. The park is well maintained  and the amenities are very clean.  Great location and easy access to walks along the waterfront and different food venues. Certainly value for money.|Highly recommend.

We spent 1 week in the park.We are from hungary,and we Traveled a lot around Europe.This park has a high standards .All the facilities very clean.Only problem its too far from ,us so we cant visit very often

Great place for a getaway or relax
Beautiful location and great service

Eliza has a lot of pride in her job,always has a smile on her face and the showers,toilets,BBQ area always spotless.the grounds were also kept clean and tidy.the security as in back fence could do with a improvement on several occasions young boys jumping over supposedly to cut through.hmmm
Not very good lighting either.
Excellent service and polite staff, convenient location well maintained.

This is a small campground quite nicely located in Morton Bay. It has a bus stop close by that can take you into Brisbane. Unfortunately the campground boundary is on  a 4 lane busy street and it was so loud. |Facilities were ok but the constant noise really unbearable for us.
Très propre. À échelle humaine et proche de BRISBANE
Great spot. Bookings essential
Visited some friends there, this place looks great.

Wonderful welcome from lovely lady on reception (Alyssa?) at this tidy small camp. Right on busy road so expect to be disturbed!
Immaculate facilities and free hot showers.

If you’re sensitive to traffic noise make sure you are pitched away from the main road cause it’s going  mad till 23:00 quietens down a bit then the petrolheads and motorcyclists are roaring past  presumably going home. Barely slept before it all started up again from 6:00am if you can put up with this it is a clean site with good facilities. I can’t give any more than three stars because of the traffic noise.
Having said this I should have realised, the site being on the main road. A lesson learned!
Friendly and helpful staff. Nice amenities.

Great park well managed and clean
All the staff are extremely helpful
I Would recommend this park great place to stay
Thank you
Great Location, nice and quiet, clean amenities and friendly staff

Just had Easter at Bells & while happy-ish we have some concerns.
1. Rats! Dozens of them around the rubbish bin area. It's little wonder when you look at the state of the area overall.
2. Around our site was quite overgrown, maybe a sign for the rats, & long overdue for cutting back. Being wet it was a breeding ground for mozzies & other pesky bugs. Our van got smashed with little ant-like insects that were had to control.
3. 2 of 3 washing machines were out of order the whole weekend, meaning we had to do laundry at 1st light. The 1 operating machine actually made some clothes worse & needed hand washing.

Appreciate the weather's been bad but other parks we visited recently have up to scratch.

This park has alot of potential, but needs a good makeover.
Yep..very good service with quick booking response...got my view with reliable hookups and reasonable rates..highly recommended

Booked for  2 1/2 week  beach side holiday,  we were put down  the back of the park, miles from anywhere,  after  seeing  sites  closer to the  beach  my wife  asked a young girl in the  office  for a change to  better site, she was rude and certainty not interested,  we left after  a week  never to return.

I paid $35 a night for a powered site in Feb, 2024 which is way below market price.
Bathrooms and toilets are squeaky clean.
Staff are very helpful and eager to help.

To some, it may seem a bit tight but it's still more spacious than those sites in big names like BigFour and NRMA.

Truly bang for the bucks!

Power gone out twice they don’t care
Loss of food in
Fridges
Pay good money for sites get better service elsewhere
$300 worth of groceries down the drain

It is located close to a major road. The traffic noise is so loud that it is difficult to sleep. There are so many mosquitoes and flies that it is difficult to open the door. The staff is always away and does not answer the phone, making it difficult to confirm inquiries. In Australia It was the worst campsite I've ever stayed in. It's a campsite I never want to go to again.
And the price was so high.
Powered sites great position.

Excellent location!
A park and lovely walking paths around the water edge are just across the road. Nice just to sit and relax.
There is an opp shop and a fish & chip shop on the corner, which was convenient.
We had a week at this park, the staff was lovely, amenities were  nice and clean, the park grounds were well maintained.
We found the park was a little tight with caravans. We have a big rig.(Avida- LONGREACH)
But we were very lucky to fit in ok.
We would definitely recommend this caravan park and will return.
We love this area!!!!
Clean and quiet place , close to the water , staff are friendly, love it
Awesome little caravan park in a great location.
Perfect spot for a few days near Brisbane. Clean amenities and friendly staff. Nice and quiet. To visit Brisbane: drive to Dock D and take the Citycat, simple.
Close to everything great for kids
My first "real" caravan park. Thank you for everything
Love this place.  Beautiful views  , clean,  probably the best we've stayed at
Good if you can find staff to help, rang the office for 2days no response until the 3rd day.

As a first time solo traveller (female)  I couldn’t have asked for better. The park and staff made me feel safe and welcome. BCP’s location, cleanliness and staff were fantastic. I had the only tent site and access to communal fridge, and kitchen  (heaven for a tenter !)

The service was second to none, fellow travellers in park were all friendly and courteous. Just the right size park with 62 sites. The parks caretakers, Ronnie and Phill very proactive, professional,  with hearts of gold.

Situated directly across from Bells Beach at Clontarf with stunning views (Day and night)  of Port of Brisbane & CBD. Public transport at your door and a great fish and chip shop on corner. Shops an easy walk along the beach.

Highly recommend. By far my best camping experience. Will definitely be back.
Great location very clean readily accommodated our site change due to our large van friendly atmosphere

Tenille and Eliza were great to deal with.Very clean facilities.
Stay was great.

We enjoy Bells Caravan Park because it is quiet with well laid out caravan sites and clean amenities and for it's location close to Brisbane allowing us to catch up with family. It is just across the road from Morton Bay walking paths with magnificent views of the Bay and Woody Point.

This small caravan park is next to the sea and well positioned for nearby shops and walks along the seafront. We have been here many times and always enjoy the clean and tidy way that this caravan park is presented. Although the facilities are fairly old they are always very clean and the staff are very pleasant and helpful at all times.
Love this little sleepy hollow straight opposite beautiful beaches and minutes from town

Stayed here before on site 53 and didn't have a problem with traffic noise somehow BUT let me assure you if you stay on sites 37-47 you are literally 6m away from a 4 lane VERY busy road. Booked 6 nights, wife broke down after 3 and we simply had to leave as it was horrible, no refund considered as I suspect this would happen often. BTW, we are in a Jayco Swan Outback so caravans may be different. Facilities clean, but apart from the bowls club and a good fish and chip shop very close by, there really is nothing else near here. I'd check Scarborough out next time, its quiet and had shops near it

We had a couple of short stays on this lovely site during our recent trip to Australia.
Superb location, modern and well maintaine ameneties and very helpful staff.
Special mention for Jess and Taylor who both went above and beyond to help with problems we had both with our mobile phones and with a breakdown with our rental camper, they were both brilliant. Thank you.

We've stayed here on many occasions. Small but clean and the staff are excellent.  The amenities are always clean and well maintained. I particularly love the location and I quite often ride my bike all the way to Sandgate Aquatic Centre just to admire the beautiful scenery.
Very nice and clean. Shower spaces quite small but ok. Not overcrowded. Beaut outdoor kitchen and laundry 👌
Nice small and clean campground with camp kitchen.
By the Water, Great Spot, very clean and tidy Caravan Park & very good rates for a powered site
